threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'overground' instead of just 'the train'—everyone else knows it’s all the same once you’re off the Tube.
Caught a night bus from Brixton and overheard two lads discussing how they swear the old lady on the 35 talks to the ghosts that haunt the route.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a tango band playing in the park while kids were coloring with chalk on the pavement. Pure Neukölln magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that opens at midnight and has a line of people debating whether to dip their döner in curry ketchup.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they casually avoid eye contact with the guy in the dog costume outside the BART, like it's just another Tuesday.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today that was half a church choir, half a comedy show. Who knew the 38 had such talent on board?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to Park Güell, but locals know that the true magic lies in the lesser-known green spaces like Jardins de la Vil.la Cecilia, where silence reigns.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them easily—they're the ones queuing for hours at La Padrina while the locals grab takeout from the tiny places nearby.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think you can get a seat on the 38 during rush hour. Bless your heart, you'll learn soon enough.
The 133 bus at 3 AM had a group singing old Spice Girls songs, and the driver joined in—only in Brixton would that happen!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Last week, I saw a busker playing a didgeridoo while a couple danced salsa on the sidewalk. That's when the Neukölln spirit hit me.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you see buskers with full sound systems setting up right next to a playground. It's like the kids are at a mini festival while they play.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they flinch at the sound of a BART announcement—like the one that made us all delay our dinner plans again.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: I once saw a guy play a trumpet solo on the bus while balancing a pizza box. The driver just sighed and kept driving. Classic Muni.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think we all speak perfect English, but many locals only chat in Catalan or Castellano. That language barrier can surprise them.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They always seem baffled by the lunch rush at 2 PM, standing outside a closed café, while locals are already siesta-ing.
